vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a severe degradation of the expected biome. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visually identifiable as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by the white rabbit anchor; the plant itself is absent or entirely necrotic.

plant_audit.P1: No visible foliage or stem structure. Pot appears empty.
plant_audit.P2: No visible foliage. Soil surface is dark and featureless.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor remains, but the Pothos specimen is missing/dead.
plant_audit.P4: No visible foliage. Shared pot space with P2 is devoid of plant matter.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Uniformly dark, suggesting high moisture or lack of light reflection. No visible fungal blooms, but significant lack of organic vitality.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments are present in the foreground pot, serving as a persistent, non-biological intervention.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: The desk surface is clear of organic debris, indicating a sterile or neglected environment.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial state shows a single green sprout/stem in the foreground pot.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Gradual loss of turgor and eventual disappearance of the green sprout.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Complete absence of biological material. The biome has transitioned to a dormant/dead state.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of total collapse. There is zero evidence of photosynthetic activity or plant survival across all four registered pots.
anomalies: Eggshell fragments in the foreground pot (New Intervention).
Total absence of expected flora (Systemic Loss).
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical setup. Starting from the earliest image, which showed a singular, struggling sprout, the sequence documents a steady decline into total necrosis or removal of all plant matter. By the current timestamp, the pots contain only soil and inorganic debris (eggshells). The white rabbit anchor remains, but the Pothos it was meant to accompany is gone. The environment is currently devoid of life.
